title: Glazed Croatian Katsuobushi Ramen
description: A silky, rich ramen infused with caramelized katsuobushi, designed as a hangover cure with a Croatian twist. Paleo-friendly and served in a classic bento-box for a satisfying al dente finish.
instructions: Bring vegetable broth to a gentle simmer in a saucepan.
Add buckwheat ramen noodles to the broth and cook until al dente, about 6 minutes.
While noodles cook, heat sesame oil in a small skillet over medium heat and stir‑fry the ginger‑garlic mix for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add katsuobushi to the skillet, searing it lightly until the flakes caramelize, about 1 minute.
Pour the maple syrup and fish sauce over the katsuobushi, whisking to create a glossy glaze that coats the flakes.
Drain the noodles, reserving a cup of broth, and return them to the pot.
Add the caramelized katsuobushi glaze to the noodles, stirring to coat evenly.
Pour in half the reserved broth to achieve a silky, slightly soupy consistency, then stir in lime juice.
Divide the ramen into a bento‑box, topping each serving with julienned carrot, sliced cucumber, and a boiled egg half.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and serve immediately while hot.
